119.content: I want to tell you a true story.
It happened to a friend of mine a year ago.
While my friend, George, was reading in bed, two thieves climbed into his kitchen.
After they had entered the house, they went into the dining room.
It was very dark, so they turned on a torch Suddenly, they heard a voice behind them.
The thieves dropped the torch and ran away as quickly as they could.
George heard the noise and came downstairs quickly.
He turned on the light, but he couldn't see anyone.
But George's parrot, Henry, was still there.

121.content: Would you put it on, please?

121.speaker: MANAGER
121.content: Is this the man that you served, Caroline?

124.content: I served her I served him I saw them yesterday.
He is the man She is the woman They are the men I served yesterday.
These are the books This is the book This is the kitten I bought yesterday.

128.content: It can't be my new hat.
It must be my old one.

129.content: husband to do next time?

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: Look, Gary!

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: That policeman's waving to you.

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: He wants you to stop.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: Where do you think you are?

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: On a race track?

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: You must have been driving at seventy miles an hour.

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: I can't have been.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: I was doing eighty when I overtook you.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: Didn't you see the speed limit?

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: I'm afraid I didn't, officer.

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: I must have been dreaming

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: He wasn't dreaming, officer.

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: I was telling him to drive slowly.

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: That's why I didn't see the sign.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: Let me see your driving licence.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: I won't charge you this time.

129.speaker: POLICEMAN
129.content: But you'd better not do it again!

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: Thank you.

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: I'll certainly be more careful.

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: I told you to drive slowly, Gary.

129.speaker: GARY
129.content: You always tell me to drive slowly, darling.

129.speaker: ANN
129.content: Well, next time you'd better take my advice!

130.content: It can't have been my new hat.
It must have been my old one.
He can't have been a dentist.
He must have been a doctor.
He can't have been the youngest.
He must have been the oldest.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: My wife wants to go to Egypt.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: I'd like to go there, too.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: We can't make up our minds.

131.speaker: MARTIN
131.content: Will you travel by sea or by air?

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: It may be cheaper, but it takes a long time.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: My wife always worries too much.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: Who's going to look after the dog?

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: Who's going to look after the house?

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: Who's going to look after the garden?

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: We have this problem every year.

131.speaker: GARY
131.content: In the end, we stay at home and look after everything!

132.content: He may be in his room.
He may go to the cinema.

133.speaker: REPORTER
133.content: Are you going to make another?

133.speaker: MISS MARSH
133.content: I don't wanttomakeanotherfilm for a long time.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: AREN MARSH By our reporter, Alan Jones.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: NEWS Karen Marsh arrived at London Airport today.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: She was wearing a blue dress and a mink coat.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: She told me she had just made a new film.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: She said she was not going to make another.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: She said she was going to retire.

133.speaker: KATE
133.content: She told reporters she felt very tired and didn't want to make another film for a long time.'

If he falls, If it rains tomorrow, he'll hurt himself.

he can buy a new one.

141.content: Last week, my four-year-old daughter, Sally.
was invited to a children's party.
I decided to take her by train.
Sally was very excited because she had never travelled on a train before.
She sat near the window and asked questions about everything she saw.
Suddenly, a middle-aged lady got on the train and sat opposite Sally.
Sally did not answer, but looked at her curiously.
The lady was dressed in a blue coat and a large, funny hat.
After the train had left the station, She then began to make up her face.
"Why are you doing that?' Sally asked.
'To make myself beautiful,' the lady answered.
She put away her compact and smiled kindly.
'But you are still ugly,' Sally said.
Sally was amused, but I was very embarrassed!

143.content: I live in a very old town which is surrounded by beautiful woods.
It is a famous beauty spot.
On Sundays, hundreds of people come from the city to see our town and to walk through the woods.
Visitors have been asked to keep the woods clean and tidy.
Litter baskets have been placed under the trees.
but people still throw their rubbish everywhere.
Last Wednesday, I went for a walk in the woods.
What I saw made me very sad.
I counted seven old cars and three old refrigerators.
The litter baskets were empty and the ground was covered with pieces of paper, cigarette ends, old tyres, empty bottles and rusty tins.
Among the rubbish, I found a sign which said, Anyone who leaves litter in these woods will be prosecuted !'
